title: China-to-Colombia EV Charger Compliance Gap Matrix
provenance: AI-compiled, multi-model cross-checked, not human-verified. Informational only; confirm with the official regulator and a qualified professional. Not legal/certification/customs/market-access advice.

rows.target_requirement: Colombia uses a 60 Hz electricity system. EV chargers designed and tested for China should be checked for 60 Hz input operation, protection timing, metering accuracy, thermal behavior, EMC or harmonic evidence, and nameplate values before Colombian installation.
rows.china_equivalent: China domestic EVSE is commonly configured and tested for 50 Hz grid service. A 50 Hz-only type test, nameplate, firmware profile, or EMC report should not be assumed to cover Colombia's 60 Hz service.
rows.gap: Provide a 60 Hz model configuration, firmware version matrix, input-stage validation, thermal validation at rated load, protection and metering checks, power-quality evidence, and Colombian nameplate and manual values that match the imported SKU.

mandatory_status.target: Mandatory as a practical design and installation condition for equipment connected to Colombia's 60 Hz grid; exact test evidence is project and conformity-route dependent
mandatory_status.cn: China domestic baseline is generally 50 Hz; 60 Hz validation is export-market and project-specific

rows.verdict: [INFORMATIONAL] Re-check the charger as a 60 Hz Colombia grid product. A 50 Hz China configuration is not enough evidence without 60 Hz settings, tests, and commissioning controls.

source.publisher: Ministerio de Minas y Energía de Colombia

rows.target_requirement: IEC 62196 covers plugs, socket-outlets, vehicle connectors, and vehicle inlets for conductive EV charging. Colombia projects should verify the vehicle fleet, connector family, public-access expectation, and any IEC 62196 requirement in the tender or operator specification instead of assuming China GB/T connector compatibility.
rows.china_equivalent: China commonly uses GB/T 20234 for EV conductive charging connection devices, including GB/T 20234.1-2023 for general requirements and GB/T 20234.3-2023 for DC charging interfaces. GB/T AC and DC interfaces are separate connector systems from IEC 62196 Type 2 or CCS2 implementations unless a dual-standard design or adapter is specifically validated.
rows.gap: Prepare connector drawings, pinout, mating-cycle ratings, locking behavior, IP rating, temperature-rise evidence, cable ratings, and local vehicle compatibility evidence. For DC chargers, evaluate the connector gap separately from the communication-protocol gap.

mandatory_status.target: Project, interoperability, customer, or installation dependent unless a Colombia rule, tender, utility requirement, or operator specification expressly specifies the connector standard
mandatory_status.cn: GB/T 20234 is the common China-market connector baseline; mandatory status depends on Chinese product and project requirements

rows.verdict: [INFORMATIONAL] A GB/T 20234 connector can be a Colombia market-fit risk unless the project explicitly accepts it or the charger is redesigned and validated for the connector family expected at the site.

rows.target_requirement: Colombia grid connection for EV charging infrastructure is handled through the electricity-sector regulatory framework and the local distribution utility. High-power chargers may require load studies, service-capacity confirmation, metering, protection coordination, power-quality evidence, and utility acceptance before operation.
rows.china_equivalent: China project files may include State Grid or China Southern Grid connection data, GB/T charger tests, and domestic commissioning records. Those documents do not transfer automatically to a Colombian distribution utility connection review.
rows.gap: Provide the Colombian site single-line diagram, maximum demand, simultaneity assumptions, harmonic and flicker evidence, protection settings, metering plan, transformer loading, grounding design, and Spanish commissioning records for the local utility and installer.

mandatory_status.target: Mandatory for energized grid connection and operation where the distribution utility or applicable CREG framework requires connection review, metering, protection, or power-quality acceptance
mandatory_status.cn: Chinese grid-connection records are project-specific and not accepted as Colombian distribution-utility approval

rows.verdict: [INFORMATIONAL] Treat grid connection as a Colombian site approval issue. China commissioning records help engineering review but do not replace the local utility connection and acceptance process.

source.publisher: Comisión de Regulación de Energía y Gas (CREG)

rows.requirement: China GB/T 18487 Baseline Versus Colombia Evidence
rows.target_requirement: Colombia-facing EV charger evidence should be organized around RETIE scope, local installation acceptance, utility connection, and any IEC standards or connector family required by the project. GB/T 18487 reports are useful engineering inputs but should be converted into a Colombia evidence matrix rather than presented as direct approval.
target_standards: IEC 62196 where connector evidence is required
rows.china_equivalent: GB/T 18487.1-2023 is the common China conductive charging system baseline and is often paired with GB/T 20234.1-2023 connector evidence. It helps identify functions, protection, communication, and charging-mode design, but it does not replace Colombia market, installation, connector, 60 Hz, or utility decisions.
rows.gap: Build a crosswalk showing each GB/T 18487 and GB/T 20234 test item, the corresponding Colombia or IEC project requirement, the design delta, and the evidence owner. Flag Spanish labels, RETIE scope, 60 Hz operation, connector variant, utility connection, and commissioning evidence.

mandatory_status.target: Informational unless a Colombia authority, RETIE route, project specification, customer, or utility expressly accepts or requires the referenced standard mapping
mandatory_status.cn: GB/T 18487 and GB/T 20234 are common China-market technical baselines; mandatory status depends on Chinese certification catalogue and deployment context

rows.verdict: [INFORMATIONAL] GB/T 18487 evidence is a starting point, not the Colombia answer. Convert China reports into a Colombia evidence matrix and close RETIE, connector, 60 Hz, grid, language, and commissioning gaps before shipment.

source.publisher: State Administration for Market Regulation / Standardization Administration of China

rows.target_requirement: IEC 61851-1 defines general requirements for EV conductive charging systems, including EVSE operating characteristics, connection to the EV, charging modes, and electrical-safety provisions. In Colombia, IEC 61851 is best treated as a project or buyer evidence baseline unless RETIE, a tender, a utility, or a conformity route expressly incorporates it.
rows.china_equivalent: China's common conductive charging system baseline is GB/T 18487. It is directionally related to IEC 61851 but should be mapped clause-by-clause before it is used for Colombia-facing design or procurement evidence.
rows.gap: Map charging mode, AC/DC rating, control pilot behavior, protective earthing, residual-current protection, automatic reclosing, marking, manuals, and installation interface from GB/T evidence to IEC 61851 and any Colombia project requirements.

mandatory_status.target: Not automatically mandatory as a standalone Colombia product certificate unless incorporated by RETIE, contract, utility requirement, tender, or project specification
mandatory_status.cn: GB/T 18487 use is common for Chinese EV charging-system design and testing; legal mandatory status depends on Chinese catalogue and project context

rows.verdict: [INFORMATIONAL] Use IEC 61851 as a Colombia-facing evidence map where the buyer, project, utility, or RETIE route calls for IEC alignment; do not present GB/T 18487 as a direct substitute.

rows.requirement: Ley 1964 EV Promotion Is Not Product Approval
rows.target_requirement: Ley 1964 of 2019 promotes the use of electric vehicles in Colombia through incentives and public-policy measures. It supports the EV market context, but it should not be read as a charger product certificate, RETIE conformity decision, or distribution-utility grid-connection approval.
rows.china_equivalent: China EV charger exporters may be familiar with policy support for new-energy vehicles and charging infrastructure. Policy incentives are separate from product safety, connector compatibility, electrical installation, and grid-connection evidence.
china_standards: China new-energy vehicle and charging-infrastructure policy measures
rows.gap: Do not cite Ley 1964 as market-access evidence for a specific charger model. Keep the commercial policy memo separate from the technical compliance file covering RETIE, IEC or GB/T mapping, connector selection, 60 Hz validation, and utility connection.

mandatory_status.target: Informational for charger product compliance; policy benefits or public-infrastructure obligations may matter commercially but do not replace technical approvals
mandatory_status.cn: Chinese EV policy materials are commercial context and do not replace Colombia technical compliance evidence

rows.verdict: [INFORMATIONAL] Ley 1964 is useful market context, not a charger approval route. Keep incentive analysis separate from RETIE, grid, connector, and safety evidence.

source.publisher: Secretaría General del Senado de Colombia

rows.requirement: RETIE Electrical Safety and Installation Scope
rows.target_requirement: Colombia's RETIE is the main electrical-installation safety framework for electrical products and installations in scope. EV charger projects should confirm whether the charger, protection devices, conductors, enclosure, marking, installation design, inspection, and energized installation fall under RETIE requirements before shipment or commissioning.
rows.china_equivalent: China-market EVSE files commonly rely on GB/T 18487, GB/T 20234, China electrical-safety component certificates, and domestic installation drawings. These are useful technical inputs but do not replace a Colombia RETIE scope and installation review.
rows.gap: Prepare Spanish technical documentation, nameplate and warning labels, RETIE scope analysis, protection and earthing design, component certificates, installation drawings, and inspection evidence. Treat product evidence and site installation evidence as separate workstreams.

mandatory_status.target: Mandatory where the product or installation is within RETIE scope; final scope and conformity route should be confirmed with the Colombia importer, inspector, and qualified electrical professional
mandatory_status.cn: China GB/T and domestic installation evidence may be required by Chinese product or project context but is not a Colombia RETIE approval

rows.verdict: [INFORMATIONAL] Start with RETIE scope and installation review. A China GB/T charger test report is supporting evidence, not a substitute for Colombia RETIE compliance where RETIE applies.

source.publisher: Ministerio de Minas y Energía de Colombia
